channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Braskem Presses on with Petrochemicals Growth Plans
item.description: Braskem, the heavily indebted Brazilian petrochemical giant seeking an extrajudicial recovery, is continuing its push to increase production even as prices fall in the wake of the peace deal between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: G7 Takes Aim at China’s Grip on Critical Minerals
item.description: The leaders of the G7 have created a strategic alliance on critical minerals in a coordinated effort to break China’s oversized control of the metals and minerals and rare earth elements crucial to the defense…

item.title: Energy Security, Not Climate Goals, Is Now Driving the Clean Power Boom
item.description: While ships are beginning to trickle through the Strait of Hormuz after months of near-total closure, it will be a very long time before the effects of this year’s energy crisis fade from the global economy – if they…

item.title: Iraq Is Keeping Its Syria Oil Route—Even If Hormuz Reopens
item.description: Nobody, especially not Iraq, wants to be caught relying on Hormuz ever again. Iraq is preparing to export crude oil and naphtha through Syria's Mediterranean port of Baniyas, expanding an emergency workaround that…

item.title: China Is Taking Its AI Boom Under the Sea
item.description: The data centre industry has taken off in the last few years, as tech companies look to develop the massive computing power needed to run complex operations, such as artificial intelligence. Thousands of data centres…

item.title: Why Small Modular Reactors Are Becoming a National Security Priority
item.description: For decades, energy policy in Washington was debated on the basis of economics, climate change, and domestic politics. That era is over. The United States is entering a period where energy security must be recognized as…

item.title: Scatec Says Solar Project Can Save Egypt $400MM from LNG
item.description: Scatec's Obelisk solar and battery storage project in Egypt can save the country as much as $400 million a year in liquefied natural gas imports, according to CEO Terje Pilskog.

item.title: The U.S. States Leading the Backlash Against Data Centers
item.description: Several U.S. states are concerned about the rapid expansion of data centres, as consumers pressure legislators to address rising utility bills and other energy concerns. Data centres are expected to be a major consumer…

item.title: Oil Rises as Iran Talks Are Delayed
item.description: Oil prices rose as delayed US-Iran negotiations and slower tanker traffic through Hormuz renewed supply concerns.

item.title: Solar Is the Cheapest Power in History, But States Are Retreating From It
item.description: A high-stakes push-and-pull is taking place in the United States clean energy sector as state-level laws, federal-level policy, and that infamous invisible hand all tug in different directions. A staggering amount of…

item.title: Why Lunar Helium-3 Mining Still Can't Compete With Earth
item.description: When it comes to Helium-3, the biggest cost divide is between Earth and the Moon. Potential sources range from tritium decay and terrestrial helium wells on Earth to lunar regolith on the Moon. Today, Earth-based…

item.title: Iran Delays Permanent Peace Talks after Lebanon Clashes
item.description: Iran delayed the start of negotiations over a permanent peace deal with the US after fighting intensified in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Vitol Supplies Emergency Fuel to Zambia in IMF-Opposed Deal
item.description: Vitol is supplying diesel to Zambia via exclusive access to a pipeline that lasts through September, in an emergency arrangement the International Monetary Fund has urged authorities to end.

item.title: The Real Reason BP Is Retreating From the North Sea
item.description: BP is still considering a sale of all or part of its UK upstream portfolio, which could fetch around £2 billion ($2.7 billion), though talks of a sale to Ithaca Energy fell through earlier this month, according to…

item.title: Venture Global Bags New LNG Supply Contract from EnBW
item.description: Venture Global executed new agreements to supply about 0.82 million metric tons per annum of U.S. liquefied natural gas for 5 years to Germany's EnBW.

item.title: Energy Transfer to Expand Texas NGL Export Terminal
item.description: 'The project will increase ethane export capacity at Nederland by 240,000 barrels per day, along with 55,000 bpd of additional LPG capacity'.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Truce Gives Oil Markets a Reason to Pause
item.description: The Middle East's latest peace deal just got a peace deal of its own. Israel and Hezbollah agreed Friday to halt fighting in southern Lebanon after days of escalating clashes threatened to derail the fragile US-Iran…

item.title: Var Energi to Proceed with Balder Next Project
item.description: Expected to begin production 2027, the Jotun FPSO tieback project will develop 86 million barrels of oil equivalent gross proven and probable reserves.

item.title: Russia Is Rationing Fuel in Moscow After Drone Strikes
item.description: Russia, one of the world's largest oil producers and exporters, is now rationing gasoline in its own capital. After months of increasingly successful Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil infrastructure, gasoline…

item.title: Valeura Energy concludes Nong Yao drilling campaign in Gulf of Thailand
item.description: Valeura Energy has completed an eight-well drilling programmme on the Nong Yao field, located in the offshore Gulf of Thailand.
